groups.title: Inflation
groups.blurb: Three ways the U.S. measures inflation — consumer prices (CPI), producer prices (PPI), and the PCE deflator.

indicators.title: US Consumer Price Index
indicators.subtitle: Headline and core CPI, components, and energy prices — sourced live from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.
indicators.card: Headline & core CPI, monthly change, components, energy prices. Source: BLS.

indicators.title: US Producer Price Index
indicators.subtitle: Final-demand prices producers receive — headline, core, goods, services, foods, energy. Source: BLS.
indicators.card: Final-demand prices producers receive — headline, core, goods, services. Source: BLS.

indicators.title: PCE Price Index
indicators.subtitle: The Fed's preferred inflation gauge — headline, core, services, and supercore. Source: BEA.
indicators.card: The Fed's preferred gauge — headline, core, services, supercore. Source: BEA.

indicators.title: US Labor Market
indicators.subtitle: Payrolls, unemployment, wages, hours, participation, and JOLTS — sourced live from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.
indicators.card: Payrolls, unemployment, JOLTS, wage growth, participation.

groups.title: Housing
groups.blurb: Sales, permits & starts, prices, mortgage activity.

indicators.title: US Existing Home Market
indicators.subtitle: Existing home sales, median price, inventory, Case-Shiller HPI, and the 30-year mortgage rate — sourced live from FRED.
indicators.card: Existing home sales, median price, inventory, Case-Shiller HPI, mortgage rate. Source: FRED.

indicators.title: US New Home Market
indicators.subtitle: New home sales, prices, inventory by stage of construction, months supply, regional sales, and the NAHB Housing Market Index — sourced live from the US Census Bureau, FRED, and NAHB.
indicators.card: New home sales, median & average price, inventory, regional sales, NAHB sentiment. Source: Census, FRED, NAHB.

indicators.title: US Building Permits & Housing Starts
indicators.subtitle: Permits authorized and units started — total, single-family, and multi-family (with the 2-4 and 5+ unit breakdown). Sourced live from the US Census Bureau via FRED; SAAR.
indicators.card: Permits & starts — total, single-family, multi-family, MoM by type, permits-to-starts ratio. Source: Census/FRED.

indicators.title: US Mortgage Activity
indicators.subtitle: Application volume, rates & spreads, the “golden handcuff,” and stress indicators — sourced live from the MBA, Freddie Mac, the Federal Reserve, and BEA.
indicators.card: MBA applications, 30Y/15Y rates, spread, the golden handcuff, delinquency, debt, affordability. Source: MBA, Freddie Mac, Fed, BEA.

groups.title: GDP
groups.blurb: Real GDP, contributions, profits, productivity, and the Conference Board leading index.

indicators.title: Gross Domestic Product
indicators.subtitle: Real GDP, component contributions, corporate profits, productivity, and GDP vs GDI — sourced live from the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis, BLS, and FRED.
indicators.card: Real GDP growth, component contributions, corporate profits, productivity, GDP vs GDI.

indicators.title: Index of Leading Economic Indicators
indicators.subtitle: The Conference Board's composite Leading Economic Index (LEI) — the standard forward-looking gauge of the U.S. business cycle. Index level and the six-month annualized growth rate, with NBER recessions shaded.
indicators.card: Conference Board LEI — index level and 6-month growth rate, with recession shading. Source: The Conference Board.

groups.title: Consumer
groups.blurb: Retail sales, income & spending, sentiment & confidence.

indicators.title: Income, Spending & Debt
indicators.subtitle: How US households are earning, spending, saving, and borrowing — income & consumption (nominal and real), saving rate, interest payments, consumer credit, revolving debt, and 90+ day delinquencies. Source: BEA via FRED; NY Fed.
indicators.card: Income & consumption, saving rate, interest payments, consumer credit, revolving debt, delinquencies.

indicators.title: Retail Sales & Confidence
indicators.subtitle: Retail sales (total / ex-MV / control), sector contributions across 12 NAICS categories, UMich Consumer Sentiment, and the Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index — Census, FRED, UMich, Conference Board.
indicators.card: Retail sales (total/ex-MV/control), sector contributions, UMich sentiment, Conference Board confidence.

groups.title: Rates
groups.blurb: Treasury yields, the curve, credit spreads, equities, commodities.

indicators.title: Treasury Yields, Fed Funds & Credit Spreads
indicators.subtitle: The shape of the yield curve, the level of policy rates, the market's read on inflation, and the price of corporate credit — sourced live from FRED. Daily, in percent unless noted.
indicators.card: Yield curve, 10Y, 2s10s, Fed funds vs 10Y, real yields & breakevens, IG vs HY credit spreads.

indicators.title: US Equities — Indices, Volatility & Valuation
indicators.subtitle: The four major US benchmarks (S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow, Russell 2000), the VIX, the S&P's drawdown from its high, and an economy-wide valuation gauge (Wilshire 5000 / after-tax profits).
indicators.card: S&P 500, major indices rebased, VIX, drawdown, the Wilshire/profits valuation gauge, Nasdaq vs Russell.

indicators.title: Commodities — Metals & Energy
indicators.subtitle: Gold and silver, the gold/silver ratio, WTI vs Brent crude, Henry Hub natural gas, platinum, and an energy-vs-metals composite. Daily spot via Kitco/MetalPriceAPI and FRED.
indicators.card: Gold & silver, gold/silver ratio, WTI vs Brent, natural gas, platinum, energy-vs-metals composite.

groups.title: Industry
groups.blurb: Industrial production, factory orders, ISM PMIs.

indicators.title: Manufacturing & Production
indicators.subtitle: Industrial production, capacity utilization, factory orders, capital-goods shipments, and electricity generation — the hard data on whether the productive economy is expanding. Sourced live from the Fed G.17, Census M3 (via FRED), BLS CPI, and the EIA.
indicators.card: Industrial production, capacity utilization, factory orders, durable goods, capital-goods shipments, electricity. Source: Fed, Census, EIA.

indicators.title: Industry Surveys — ISM, Cass & NFIB
indicators.subtitle: The forward-looking layer on top of the hard data: ISM Manufacturing & Services PMIs and sub-indices, the Cass Freight Index, and NFIB small-business optimism & uncertainty. PMIs above 50 = expansion.
indicators.card: ISM Manufacturing & Services PMIs, components, Cass Freight, NFIB optimism & uncertainty. Source: ISM, Cass, NFIB.

groups.title: Gov't
groups.blurb: Federal debt, fiscal flows, money supply, the Fed balance sheet.

indicators.title: Government & Fiscal Policy
indicators.subtitle: The federal balance sheet, who staffs government, the cash flowing in and out of Treasury, the money supply, and the Fed's footprint — sourced live from the U.S. Treasury (Fiscal Data), the Federal Reserve via FRED, and BLS.
indicators.card: Federal debt, government employment, outlays vs receipts, M2, the Fed balance sheet, tariffs, interest expense, debt/GDP.
